channel.title: Combined Politepol Feed
channel.description: Merged feed from multiple Politepol sources

item.title: You breathe through one nostril at a time. Here’s why
Why Indonesia needs India’s BrahMos missile system
Who are Alexander brothers, US luxury real estate brokers found guilty of sex trafficking?
Food, medical check-ups & more: How Indian businessmen are helping stranded tourists in UAE
Decoding China’s latest economic plans: Tech, rare earths and more
Fact-check: Did Iranian airstrikes kill Israeli PM's brother?
Nehru’s frontier folly: How Bharat lost NWFP and gave Pakistan ‘strategic depth’
item.description: Bharat’s strategic marginalisation in Afghanistan did not begin with the rise of the Taliban in the 1990s; it actually started with a fateful decision during Partition

item.title: Beyond Trudeau’s shadow: How Mark Carney charts an India-Canada reset
item.description: Moving past the tensions of the Justin Trudeau era, Carney’s India visit marks the end of a challenging period and the beginning of a more ambitious partnership

item.title: Nepal seeks a new deal: For Balen, it’s a crown of thorns
item.description: The election result reflects deep frustration with decades of political instability, corruption, nepotism and youth unemployment. The message is clear – perform or perish

item.title: Multan’s lost Prahladpuri temple: Tracing Holi’s origins and a city’s Hindu past
item.description: Once the sacred site linked to the legend of Prahlad and the origins of Holi, Multan’s Prahladpuri Temple today stands as a forgotten reminder of the city’s erased Hindu past

item.title: Geopolitics and Dalal Street: Five lessons for retail investors in times of war
item.description: The Israel-US-Iran war is a reminder that financial markets operate within a broader geopolitical environment. While wars create short-term volatility, they rarely change the long-term fundamentals of well-managed companies or growing economies

item.title: How India clinched the T20 World Cup
item.description: India’s potent mix of aggressive batting, penetrative bowling, and shrewd captaincy propelled the Men in Blue to consecutive T20 World Cup triumphs.
